summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package executes a system-info exfiltration payload at install time via package.json (line 32) and ships a scanner.js module that performs the same harvesting on import. The install-hook one-liner pulls in child_process, os, and https, reads process.env, and POSTs the collected data to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js (lines 4-9, 142-161, 233-324) reads the filesystem, runs `whoami` and `execSync`, calls `os.hostname()`, and uses https.request/https.get plus a `curl` fallback to send host/identity data to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint. The destination is an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site unrelated to any legitimate `chain-key-validator` purpose, with no version pinning, no integrity check, and no documented relationship to the package's stated function. This matches the active-attack fingerprint: hardcoded C2 in install-time-reachable code + system-intel collection + environment-variable read + outbound POST to attacker host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
